title: Glyptocaryopsis glyptocarpa
taxonomy: Glyptocaryopsis glyptocarpa is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Burseraceae and is native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. The plant is a member of the genus Glyptocaryopsis, which is closely related to the genus Canarium. Glyptocaryopsis glyptocarpa is often classified as a subspecies of Canarium strictum. However, more recent studies suggest that it may be a distinct species within the Burseraceae family.

intro: Glyptocaryopsis glyptocarpa, also known as the Indian fir or East Himalayan fir, is a species of evergreen coniferous tree native to the eastern Himalayas and parts of southern China. The health benefits of Glyptocaryopsis glyptocarpa include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues, fever, and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include glycosides, flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Glyptocaryopsis glyptocarpa is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and fever.
